title: Novartis — Relationships
html:
Novartis — ZoomRx Relationship

Governing Thought

ZoomRx's Novartis relationship is the longest-running and most therapeutically diverse engagement in the portfolio — spanning 7 years (since 2019) across 6+ therapeutic areas and 12+ brands. The partnership was built on a single methodology: HCP-Patient Conversations, which became Novartis's go-to approach for understanding exam-room dynamics across oncology, hematology, cardiovascular, immunology, and gene therapy. The Rhapsido PET (Wave 5) represents the only deep recurring tracker, making it the revenue anchor. The relationship is wide but shallow — many brands touched once, few with multi-service engagements — creating both a vulnerability (no single brand generates enough switching cost) and an opportunity (every completed HCP-Pt study is a warm lead for PET, ATU, and other services). The SAGAN Answers demo is a strategic inflection: Novartis exploring how to ingest ZoomRx data into its internal AI analytics platform, which could either deepen integration or commoditize ZoomRx's role to raw data supplier.

Partnership Timeline

Year Milestone
2019 Relationship established — Crizanlizumab DSA PET (sickle cell disease); first ZoomRx project at Novartis
2019-2022 Early brand engagements: Tasigna (CML), Promacta (ITP)
2023 Significant expansion — Scemblix zero-cost PET, Scemblix HCP-Pt, Pelacarsen HCP-Pt, Leqvio HCP-Pt, ITP/wAIHA/PNH HCP-Pt, Zolgensma Caregiver + HCP Qual
2023 HCP-Patient Conversations established as Novartis's preferred ZoomRx methodology
2024 Continued breadth — Kisqali HCP-Pt, PNH HCP-Pt (Fabhalta), CSU PET + Digital Tracking (Remibrutinib), Pluvicto HCP-Pt, Xolair Benchmarks
2024-2025 Pluvicto PET study; Remibrutinib CSU HCP-Pt; mHSPC HCP-Pt (prostate cancer expansion)
2025-2026 Abelacimab HCP-Pt + Market Intelligence (active); Rhapsido PET reaches Wave 5; PSP Study Proposal; SAGAN Answers demo
2026 Procurement team interaction — Novartis sharing 2024-2025 project files with internal procurement, signaling centralized vendor management evaluation

Key statistic: 24 projects completed or active across 12+ brands and 6+ therapeutic areas over 7 years. Average of ~3-4 projects per year, predominantly HCP-Patient Conversations.

Why Novartis Chose ZoomRx (and Keeps Choosing)

Pillar 1: HCP-Patient Conversations — The Universal Methodology

ZoomRx's HCP-Pt methodology is what built and sustains the Novartis relationship. It has been deployed across nearly every therapeutic area at Novartis — oncology, hematology, cardiovascular, immunology, dermatology, and gene therapy — proving its versatility. No other ZoomRx methodology has this level of cross-TA adoption at Novartis.

Evidence:

  • 9+ brands have used HCP-Pt (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix, Leqvio, Pelacarsen, Fabhalta, Remibrutinib, Abelacimab, and multi-indication hematology study)
  • Cross-TA deployment demonstrates that different Novartis brand teams independently choose HCP-Pt — it is not a single buyer mandating it but organic adoption across the organization
  • The methodology uniquely captures exam-room dynamics for novel modalities (radioligand therapy, gene therapy, oral complement inhibitors) where patient communication is as much the challenge as clinical efficacy

Pillar 2: Therapeutic Versatility — One Vendor, Six TAs

ZoomRx is one of the few research partners that has demonstrated capability across Novartis's full therapeutic breadth. From rare disease gene therapy (Zolgensma) to mass-market cardiovascular (Leqvio) to niche hematology (PNH/ITP/wAIHA), ZoomRx has delivered across radically different patient populations and prescriber bases. This versatility makes ZoomRx a known quantity when a new brand team at Novartis needs HCP-Pt or tracking research.

Evidence:

  • 6 distinct therapeutic areas served: Oncology, Hematology, Cardiovascular, Immunology/Dermatology, Gene Therapy, Anticoagulation
  • New brand entries continue (Abelacimab in 2025) — the relationship is still expanding
  • Zolgensma caregiver qual demonstrates willingness to adapt methodology to novel contexts (gene therapy, caregiver perspective)

Pillar 3: Data Integration Potential — SAGAN Answers

The SAGAN Answers demo (Apr 2026) containing Rhapsido PET and Remibrutinib raw data suggests Novartis is exploring how to feed ZoomRx output into its internal AI analytics platform. This is a strategic signal: if ZoomRx data becomes embedded in Novartis's decision-support tools, the switching cost rises dramatically. But it also carries risk — if Novartis views ZoomRx as a raw data supplier rather than an insights partner, margins compress and differentiation erodes.

Evidence:

  • SharePoint folder "SAGAN Answers demo" contains Rhapsido PET Wave 5 Full Report and Remibrutinib raw data
  • Novartis has a well-publicized internal AI/data platform (SAGAN) for analytics and decision support
  • The demo format suggests early-stage exploration, not a production integration — the window to shape the partnership model is now

Proven Impact: Case Studies

1. Rhapsido (Remibrutinib) PET — Wave 5 Tracking Engagement

Challenge: Novartis needed ongoing tracking of promotional effectiveness for Rhapsido (remibrutinib) across allergists and dermatologists in the chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) market. Approach: PET tracking launched in 2024, now at Wave 5 (Apr 2026) — the longest continuous tracking engagement at Novartis. Measures message recall, physician awareness, prescribing intent, and competitive positioning vs. Xolair across dual specialist audiences. Impact: Five waves of longitudinal data provide trend analysis unavailable from any single-wave study. The dual allergist/dermatologist tracking captures how messaging lands differently across specialist types — a critical insight for field force optimization.

2. Pluvicto HCP-Patient Conversations + PET

Challenge: Pluvicto (radioligand therapy) is a novel modality for mCRPC requiring entirely new patient and HCP education approaches. Approach: ZoomRx ran HCP-Pt studies (2024 and 2025) and a PET study (2025) to understand how physicians explain radioligand therapy to patients and how promotional messages land. Impact: Captured the unique communication challenges of a first-in-class modality — HCPs describing radiation therapy to patients accustomed to oral/IV oncology agents. Multi-study depth across HCP-Pt and PET gave Novartis both qualitative and quantitative lenses on Pluvicto's communication effectiveness.

3. Multi-Indication Hematology HCP-Pt (ITP/wAIHA/PNH)

Challenge: Novartis needed to understand prescriber conversation dynamics across three related but distinct hematologic conditions (ITP, wAIHA, PNH) in a single study. Approach: ZoomRx designed a multi-indication HCP-Pt study covering all three conditions, allowing cross-condition comparison of communication patterns and patient engagement. Impact: Proved the multi-indication study model — a single research engagement covering multiple brands within a TA. This approach is more efficient for the client and creates richer cross-brand insights. Model is replicable in oncology (pan-tumor) or cardiovascular (multi-brand).

4. Zolgensma Caregiver + HCP Qual

Challenge: Zolgensma is a one-time gene therapy for SMA — a radically different treatment model where the "patient" is an infant and the decision-maker is a caregiver. Approach: ZoomRx ran dual qual studies — one with caregivers, one with HCPs — to capture both sides of the gene therapy decision-making process. Impact: Demonstrated ZoomRx's ability to adapt methodology to rare disease and gene therapy contexts. The caregiver perspective study was a novel approach not typically offered by competitor research firms.

Growth Trajectory

The relationship is expanding along three vectors:

Vector 1: Deepening Existing Tracking (Rhapsido PET)

The Rhapsido PET at Wave 5 is the account's revenue backbone. Continued waves, potential expansion to additional geographies or specialist panels, and integration with SAGAN analytics represent the lowest-risk growth path.

Vector 2: Converting HCP-Pt Studies to Multi-Service Relationships

Every completed HCP-Pt study is a warm lead for additional services on the same brand:

  • Kisqali: HCP-Pt completed — natural candidate for PET (massive >$4B brand with no active tracking)
  • Pluvicto: HCP-Pt and PET completed — natural candidate for MSL/MEE (radioligand therapy requires scientific education)
  • Fabhalta: HCP-Pt completed — natural candidate for PET (oral PNH launch, tracking needed)
  • Leqvio: HCP-Pt completed — natural candidate for PET/ATU (competitive CV market, launch tracking)
  • Scemblix: Zero-cost PET completed — conversion to paid PET is the next step

Vector 3: New Brand Entry on Untouched Mega-Brands

The three largest Novartis brands by revenue — Entresto ($7.3B), Cosentyx ($4.5B), and Kesimpta — have zero ZoomRx engagement. Any single entry on one of these brands would meaningfully move account revenue.

Competitive Threats to the Relationship

Threat 1: SAGAN and Internal AI Tools

Novartis's investment in SAGAN (internal AI/analytics) represents the most significant structural threat. If Novartis builds internal capability to analyze HCP-Pt transcripts and tracking data using AI, ZoomRx's analytical value proposition diminishes. The risk is not that Novartis stops buying data — it is that Novartis starts treating ZoomRx as a data collection utility rather than an insights partner, compressing margins and making ZoomRx substitutable.

Mitigation: Position ZoomRx as the analytical layer on top of SAGAN, not the data feed beneath it. The SAGAN demo is the moment to shape this — offer a co-designed integration where ZoomRx delivers AI-enriched insights (not raw data) that SAGAN surfaces to decision-makers. The analytical interpretation is the moat; the data is a commodity.

Threat 2: Procurement Centralization

The "2024-2025 Project Files to be shared with the Procurement Team" folder signals that Novartis procurement is evaluating vendor performance and pricing across engagements. Centralized vendor management typically leads to preferred vendor lists, price compression, and competitive RFPs replacing direct brand-team purchasing.

Mitigation: Build the case for ZoomRx's cross-TA versatility and longitudinal value (Rhapsido Wave 5 data continuity). Procurement evaluations favor vendors with proven breadth and repeat engagement — ZoomRx's 7-year, 6-TA track record is defensible. Preemptively propose a framework agreement that locks in multi-brand pricing rather than waiting for a competitive RFP.

Threat 3: Wide-but-Shallow Engagement Pattern

Most Novartis brands have a single completed HCP-Pt study and no ongoing engagement. This means switching costs are low on any individual brand — Novartis can easily substitute another vendor for the next HCP-Pt study on Kisqali or Fabhalta. Only Rhapsido (PET Wave 5) has the longitudinal depth that creates true lock-in.

Mitigation: Convert at least 2-3 brands from one-time HCP-Pt to recurring multi-service engagements (HCP-Pt + PET or HCP-Pt + ATU). Each additional service on a brand increases switching cost and revenue per brand.

Threat 4: Brand-Level Budget Decisions

Like Pfizer, Novartis brand teams have significant autonomy over vendor selection. A budget cut or re-org at any individual brand affects only that brand's ZoomRx revenue — but the pattern of single-study-per-brand means there are many small revenue streams, each individually vulnerable.

Mitigation: Diversification is already occurring (6+ TAs). Continue expanding the brand count so that no single brand represents a disproportionate share of account revenue.

Relationship Health Assessment

Dimension Rating Evidence
Trust Strong (cross-TA) 24 projects across 12+ brands over 7 years; continued new brand entries (Abelacimab 2025)
Breadth Exceptional 6+ therapeutic areas — broadest TA diversity of any ZoomRx account
Depth Shallow (except Rhapsido) Most brands have single completed HCP-Pt; only Rhapsido has multi-wave recurring engagement
Advocacy Moderate Multiple brand teams independently choose ZoomRx; no identified single above-brand champion
Strategic Positioning Evolving SAGAN demo creates integration potential; procurement evaluation creates formalization risk
Revenue Trajectory Stable with upside Rhapsido PET provides recurring base; 3 mega-brands (Entresto, Cosentyx, Kesimpta) untapped

Key Takeaway for New Principals

The Novartis account is the opposite of the typical pharma account pattern. Most accounts grow by deepening within one TA — multiple project types on the same brand, recurring trackers, annual contracts. Novartis grew by spreading wide — many brands, many TAs, predominantly a single methodology (HCP-Pt). This creates a paradox: the relationship looks broad and healthy on paper (24 projects, 6 TAs, 12+ brands) but is structurally shallow (only 1 recurring tracker, most brands touched once). The strategic imperative is to convert breadth into depth — pick 3-4 brands (Rhapsido is already there; add Kisqali, Pluvicto, and one of Entresto/Cosentyx) and build multi-service, multi-year engagements. The SAGAN integration question is the account's defining strategic fork: does ZoomRx become an embedded analytics partner or a substitutable data supplier? That decision is being made now.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
